title: Divide Whole Numbers by Fractions
contentObjective: I can divide a whole number by a fraction by multiplying by the reciprocal.
languageObjective: I can explain my steps using the words fraction, reciprocal, quotient, and Keep, Change, Flip.

turnAndTalk.question: Agent Park has 4 pounds of evidence powder and fills bags that hold 1/2 pound each. How many bags can he fill, and how do you know?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Each whole pound makes 2 half-pound bags, so 4 pounds makes 8 bags.

stems.en: 1/2 pound fits into 4 pounds a total of ___ times.
stems.es: 1/2 libra cabe en 4 libras un total de ___ veces.

stems.en: I know because each pound holds ___ bags, so ___.
stems.es: Lo sé porque cada libra contiene ___ bolsas, así que ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students see that 4 ÷ 1/2 = 8 and explain there are 2 halves in each whole pound.
turnAndTalk.extend: Why does dividing 4 by 1/2 give 8, a number larger than 4, even though you are dividing? Justify.
extendStems: Dividing 4 by 1/2 makes the answer larger because ___.
Dividing by less than one whole means ___.

turnAndTalk.question: How does 'Keep, Change, Flip' turn 6 ÷ 1/3 into a multiplication problem?
turnAndTalk.kernel: I keep the 6, change ÷ to ×, and flip 1/3 to its reciprocal 3.

stems.en: I keep ___, change the sign to ___, and flip ___ to ___.
stems.es: Mantengo ___, cambio el signo a ___, y volteo ___ a ___.
Entonces 6 ÷ 1/3 se convierte en 6 × ___, que es igual a ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students correctly flip the divisor to its reciprocal (3) and explain multiplying by the reciprocal gives the same result as dividing.
turnAndTalk.extend: Explain why multiplying by the reciprocal gives the same answer as counting how many 1/3-pieces fit into 6.
extendStems: Multiplying by the reciprocal works because ___.

turnAndTalk.question: When would dividing a whole number by a fraction help you in real life?
turnAndTalk.kernel: It tells you how many fraction-size portions fit into a whole amount.

stems.en: Dividing a whole number by a fraction is useful when ___.
stems.es: Dividir un número entero entre una fracción es útil cuando ___.

stems.en: For example, ___ pounds split into ___-pound portions makes ___.
stems.es: Por ejemplo, ___ libras divididas en porciones de ___ libra hace ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students give a realistic portion/serving scenario and set it up as whole ÷ fraction.
turnAndTalk.extend: A student says you should always multiply by the reciprocal to make the answer bigger. Critique this overgeneralization.
extendStems: This is not always true because ___.
Multiplying by a reciprocal makes the answer bigger only when ___.

vocabulary.definition: A counting number with no fraction or decimal, like 0, 1, 2, 3.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Un número de contar sin fracción ni decimal, como 0, 1, 2, 3.
vocabulary.visual: 4 is a whole number; as a fraction it is 4/1
vocabulary.cloze: A counting number like 0, 1, 2, or 3 with no fraction or decimal part is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: A number that shows part of a whole, like 3/4.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Un número que muestra una parte de un todo, como 3/4.
vocabulary.visual: 1/2 means 1 out of 2 equal parts — like cutting a sandwich in half and taking one piece
vocabulary.cloze: A number that shows part of a whole, like 3/4, is a ___.

vocabulary.definitionEs: Una fracción volteada de arriba abajo.
vocabulary.visual: The reciprocal of 2/3 is 3/2. Flip the fraction to divide: 6 ÷ 2/3 = 6 × 3/2
vocabulary.cloze: The fraction you get by flipping a fraction upside down is its ___.

vocabulary.definition: A way to divide fractions: keep the first, change ÷ to ×, flip the second.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una manera de dividir fracciones: deja la primera, cambia ÷ por ×, voltea la segunda.
vocabulary.cloze: To divide by a fraction, I ___: keep the first number, change ÷ to ×, and flip the second fraction.
The answer to a division problem is the ___.

launch.narrative: Agent Park has 4 pounds of evidence powder. Each testing bag holds exactly 1/2 pound. He needs to prepare as many testing bags as possible before the lab deadline. How many bags can he fill?
launch.contextImage: Detective at a lab bench measuring powder into small evidence bags with a scale
noticePrompts: How much total powder does Agent Park have?
How much goes into each bag?
Will the number of bags be more or less than 4?
wonderPrompts: Why does dividing by 1/2 give a number larger than 4?
How is dividing by a fraction related to multiplying?

explore.instructions: Use the number line to show how many 1/2-size jumps fit into 4.
explore.label: Show 4 ÷ 1/2 on the number line
explore.questionText: How many jumps of 1/2 does it take to get from 0 to 4?

discourse.prompt: Why does 4 ÷ 1/2 = 8? How does the number line show this?
discourse.sentenceFrame: 4 ÷ 1/2 = 8 because it takes ___ jumps of ___ to get from 0 to ___.

optional.stem: A 6-foot board is cut into 3/4-foot pieces. How many pieces?

approaching.explanation: 10 ÷ 1/4 = 10 × 4/1 = 40. There are 40 quarter-size pieces in 10 wholes.

approaching.instructions: Show how many jumps of 1/3 it takes to get from 0 to 2.
approaching.label: Model 2 ÷ 1/3 on the number line
approaching.questionText: How many jumps of 1/3 does it take to get from 0 to 2?

onLevel.label: Complete the table: Whole Number ÷ Fraction. Use Keep, Change, Flip.
Match each division expression to its quotient.

onLevel.instructions: Use the bar model to show 3 ÷ 2/3. First find how many 1/3-size pieces are in 3, then group them in pairs of 2/3.
onLevel.label: Model 3 ÷ 2/3
onLevel.totalLabel: 3 wholes = 9 thirds. Groups of 2/3: 9 ÷ 2 = 4 R 1 → 4 1/2 groups
onLevel.questionText: How many groups of 2/3 fit into 3?

extending.title: Find the Division Error
extending.correctWork: 6 × 3/2 (flip the divisor 2/3 to get 3/2, not keep it as 2/3). Then 6 × 3/2 = 18/2 = 9.
hints: In 'Keep, Change, Flip' — which fraction gets flipped?
The divisor (2/3) should become its reciprocal (3/2). Did Step 2 flip the right fraction?

extending.prompt: A pizza shop has 8 whole pizzas. Each serving is 2/3 of a pizza. How many servings can they make? Write and solve an equation, then explain why the answer is greater than 8.
extending.sentenceFrame: The equation is 8 ÷ 2/3 = ___. Using Keep, Change, Flip: 8 × 3/2 = ___. The answer is greater than 8 because ___.

extending.label: Compare: what happens when you divide the same whole number by different fractions?

connect.scenario: A detective has 10 feet of rope to section off a crime scene. Each section needs to be 1/4 of a foot long for detailed evidence markers. How many markers can the detective set up?
connect.promptQuestion: How does dividing a whole number by a fraction help the detective?
connect.prompt: The detective can set up ___ markers because 10 ÷ 1/4 = ___.
